task_completion.description: Configure a custom robots.txt to block the Travel category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A crawling tool's settings window (e.g., Screaming Frog Custom robots.txt configuration) or a script showing the rule 'Disallow: /catalogue/category/books/travel_2/' being applied to the target website.

task_completion.description: Initiate a crawl of the target website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crawling tool's main interface showing 'https://books.toscrape.com/' as the target URL with the crawl in progress or completed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Travel category URLs are successfully blocked by the custom robots.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crawl results list showing URLs containing 'travel_2' with a status indicating they are blocked (e.g., 'Blocked by robots.txt', 'Status: Blocked').

task_completion.description: Verify that other categories, such as Mystery, remain accessible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crawl results list showing URLs containing 'mystery_3' (or other non-Travel categories) with a successful status (e.g., '200 OK', 'Allowed').

task_completion.description: Export the report of URLs blocked by robots.txt to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or save dialog showing the creation or existence of 'blocked_urls.csv' inside the '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Export the internal HTML report of allowed pages to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or save dialog showing the creation or existence of 'allowed_pages.csv' inside the '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a genuine crawling tool or executed a crawling script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of an SEO spider GUI (like Screaming Frog) or terminal output showing active HTTP requests, rather than just a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the exported CSV files contain genuine crawl data.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the CSV files are opened, they should contain standard export headers (e.g., 'Address', 'Status Code', 'Status') and multiple rows of data, not just manually typed mock data.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the task by manually creating the output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal commands like 'echo "url" > blocked_urls.csv' or manual text editing of the final CSV files to fake the expected output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://books.toscrape.com/ (Returns 200 OK). Blocked path segment: 'travel_2' (Valid category). Allowed path segment example: 'mystery_3' (Valid category). Blocked export path: /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/blocked_urls.csv. Allowed export path: /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/allowed_pages.csv.
